children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Except as otherwise provided, a vessel may engage in a trade only if the vessel has been issued a certificate of documentation with an endorsement for that trade under this chapter.

children.heading: 5.—Vessels Less ThanNet Tons
children.label: b 5.—Vessels Less ThanNet Tons
children.text: A vessel of less than 5 net tons may engage in a trade without being documented if the vessel otherwise satisfies the requirements to engage in the particular trade.

children.heading: .—Barges
children.label: c .—Barges
children.text: A barge qualified to engage in the coastwise trade may engage in the coastwise trade, without being documented, on rivers, harbors, lakes (except the Great Lakes), canals, and inland waters.

children.heading: .—Permitting of nonqualified vessels to perform certain aquaculture support operations
children.label: 1 .—Permitting of nonqualified vessels to perform certain aquaculture support operations
children.text: Notwithstanding section 12113 and any other law, the Secretary of Transportation may issue a waiver allowing a documented vessel with a registry endorsement or a foreign flag vessel to be used in operations that treat aquaculture fish for or protect aquaculture fish from disease, parasitic infestation, or other threats to their health if the Secretary finds, after publishing a notice in the Federal Register, that a suitable vessel of the United States is not available that could perform those services.

children.heading: .—Prohibition
children.label: 2 .—Prohibition
children.text: Vessels operating under a waiver issued under this subsection may not engage in any coastwise transportation.
